methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $126.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $29.3B - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Waymo is a late-stage AI & Software company founded by Sebastian Thrun and Anthony Levandowski, having raised $16B+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers: 1 public company (Palantir) and 8 private AI & Software firms in similar valuation tiers, including OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, and Databricks.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to Waymo's last post-money valuation of $126.0B, applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage advancement, implying $151.2B. Peer-median analysis across the 8 same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $158.5B, with a range spanning $29.3B to $400.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneous stage, operational maturity, and capital efficiency across the AI & Software landscape.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $128.5B (low) to $182.3B (high), with a median of $154.8B. This band sits modestly above the current $126.0B post-money valuation, suggesting limited upside under conservative assumptions. Key uncertainties include peer comparability given Waymo's autonomous vehicle focus versus the generative AI concentration of the peer set, and the stability of private AI valuations amid market volatility. The analysis supports a range of $128.5B–$182.3B, centered at $154.8B.
